title: Fintech startup raises $45M Series B led by Benchmark
summary: The company announced a new $45M Series B and said it will use the capital to expand sales, compliance, and enterprise go-to-market across North America.
notes: Canonical recent fundraise with strong source and clear funding keywords.

title: AI infra company hires head of investor relations ahead of expansion
summary: The company posted a new investor relations leadership role while highlighting strong revenue growth and planned international expansion in its own press release.
notes: Fresh fundraising motion with a high-signal source and multiple scoring keywords.

title: Payments company explores secondary tender after valuation increase
summary: Bloomberg reported that the company is discussing a secondary tender and new capital options after posting strong transaction growth in the first quarter.
notes: Strong publication plus fundraising narrative; still inside the 7-day window.

title: Developer tools startup refreshes investor page with 2025 metrics
summary: The company updated its investor relations site with annual growth numbers and customer expansion metrics, but no new fundraise or investor role was announced.
notes: Aging narrative signal; interesting context, but weaker than a direct round or IR hire.

title: It has been 24 months since the company’s last funding round
summary: Crunchbase still shows the last primary round from 2024, and no fresh financing activity has been reported since then.
notes: Useful context, but stale and should not be treated as a fresh trigger.

title: Security startup launches managed browser isolation product
summary: The company launched a managed browser isolation offering for enterprise teams and published detailed pricing plus customer rollout plans on its company blog.
notes: Direct product launch with pricing and rollout details.

title: AI analytics platform ships new forecasting module
summary: Reuters reported the launch of a forecasting module aimed at mid-market operators and noted that the release expands the platform’s upsell motion.
notes: Launch keyword, high-signal source, and recent timing should score high.

title: Devtools vendor releases mobile debugging dashboard
summary: The new dashboard gives engineering teams mobile crash and session replay visibility, but the launch was announced only in a brief product update post.
notes: Aging but still meaningful launch; should remain above low.

title: Open-source maintainer publishes experimental beta feature
summary: A GitHub repository update introduced an experimental beta feature for power users, but there was no broader launch campaign or commercial packaging around it.
notes: Useful launch-like activity, but weaker source and commercial signal.

title: Startup talks about 2025 roadmap webinar
summary: The team hosted a webinar recapping last year’s roadmap themes and mentioned features that had already launched months earlier.
notes: Contains low-signal patterns and stale timing.

title: Payments API partners with Plaid for instant bank verification
summary: The new partnership gives merchants real-time bank verification and faster ACH onboarding, expanding the company’s enterprise payments footprint.
notes: Clear, recent partnership with a strong source and commercial expansion angle.

title: Cloud security vendor signs reseller partnership with AWS marketplace team
summary: Reuters reported a new reseller agreement that opens marketplace distribution and procurement pathways for enterprise buyers.
notes: Partnership plus distribution expansion is exactly the kind of high-value commercial signal we want.

title: MarTech startup integrates with HubSpot ecosystem
summary: The integration lets customers sync campaign data and audience events into HubSpot, broadening the company’s product surface for existing users.
notes: Aging but still commercially relevant partnership/integration.

title: Startup appears on partner directory page
summary: The company now appears on another vendor’s partner directory page, but there is no launch announcement, customer proof, or distribution motion attached to it.
notes: Weak partnership evidence; should not score as high despite the category.

title: Weekly recap mentions ecosystem relationships
summary: A weekly recap post mentioned that the company continues to work closely with partners, but no specific launch, customer, or signed agreement was disclosed.
notes: Low-signal pattern and stale timing should push this down.

title: Data infrastructure company opens 45 new GTM and engineering roles
summary: The company posted 45 fresh roles across sales, customer success, platform engineering, and product marketing over the last two weeks.
notes: Fresh and meaningful hiring spike, but the source quality is lower than Reuters or TechCrunch.

title: Payments startup hires VP of partnerships and enterprise sales director
summary: New senior roles were posted this week alongside multiple account executive openings, suggesting a coordinated push upmarket.
notes: Fresh hiring motion with strong titles, but still not likely to hit the high threshold.

title: Infra vendor lists 18 open roles after EMEA expansion
summary: The roles span field engineering, customer success, and regional marketing after the company entered two new EMEA markets earlier this quarter.
notes: Aging but still material hiring activity.

title: Startup still has 6 engineering openings
summary: The careers page shows six engineering openings, but no cross-functional hiring wave or leadership expansion is visible.
notes: Too small to be a major signal despite being recent-ish.

title: Podcast discusses hiring philosophy
summary: The founder appeared on a podcast discussing talent philosophy and recruiting culture, but no actual job expansion or open-role data was cited.
notes: Low-signal pattern and stale timing.

title: Cybersecurity startup appoints former Okta exec as COO
summary: The company appointed a former Okta executive as chief operating officer to lead enterprise scale-up and field operations.
notes: Fresh leadership appointment from a strong source with clear operator significance.

title: Fintech company hires CFO ahead of public-market readiness push
summary: TechCrunch reported a new CFO hire tied to audit readiness, investor reporting, and long-term IPO preparation.
notes: Leadership plus fundraising/IPO context makes this a top-tier signal.

title: MarTech platform promotes longtime product leader to GM
summary: The company promoted a senior product leader into a general manager role as it split the business into two product lines.
notes: Material org change, but not as strong as an external exec hire.

title: Former communications lead launches independent consultancy
summary: A previous communications lead left the company to launch an independent consultancy. No major change to core go-to-market or product leadership was announced.
notes: Valid leadership/personnel movement, but not top-tier.

title: Newsletter roundup mentions exec departures from last quarter
summary: A newsletter roundup briefly mentioned executive departures that had already been discussed months earlier, without any fresh company action.
notes: Low-signal pattern plus stale timing.

title: Analyst blog praises startup’s mission
summary: A short opinion post praised the company’s mission and product direction but did not cite a product launch, customer, partnership, pricing change, or financing event.
notes: Fresh but intentionally low-value because it is only opinion.

title: Weekly recap of startup ecosystem trends
summary: A weekly recap listed the company among several startups to watch, but offered no concrete event or new operating signal.
notes: Low-signal pattern with no actionable event.

title: Podcast interview about long-term category vision
summary: The CEO discussed category vision and founder advice in a podcast episode without announcing any new launch, financing, or commercial milestone.
notes: Recent but noisy and not worth surfacing prominently.

title: Listicle names startup among 10 companies to watch
summary: A listicle mentioned the startup in a broad trend piece, but the write-up had no concrete facts about customer wins, launches, or funding.
notes: The low-signal pattern should keep this firmly low.

title: Archived thought leadership post resurfaces on social media
summary: A 2025 thought leadership article circulated again, but it contains no new company event and should not be treated as new operating news.
notes: Explicit stale/noise example.
